heading: Penalties for offenses relating to stamps
label: 7271 Penalties for offenses relating to stamps
notes: Amendments1976—Pars. (2) to (4). redesignated pars. (3) and (4) as (2) and (3), respectively. Former par. (2), which related to persons who manufactured or imported and sold, or offered for sale, or caused to be manufactured or imported and sold, or offered for sale, any playing card, package, or other article without the full amount of tax being paid, was struck out.Pub. L. 94–455

children.heading: Failure to attach or cancel stamps, etc.
children.label: 1 Failure to attach or cancel stamps, etc.
children.text: Fails to comply with rules or regulations prescribed pursuant to section 6804 (relating to attachment, cancellation, etc., of stamps), unless such failure is shown to be due to reasonable cause and not willful neglect; or

children.heading: Instruments
children.label: 2 Instruments
children.text: Makes, signs, issues, or accepts, or causes to be made, signed, issued, or accepted, any instrument, document, or paper of any kind or description whatsoever without the full amount of tax thereon being duly paid; or

children.heading: Disposal and receipt of stamped packages
children.label: 3 Disposal and receipt of stamped packages
A
children.text: Transfers or receives (whether by sale, gift, or otherwise) such container knowing it to be empty or to contain such other contents; or

children.label: B
children.text: Stamps, brands, or marks such container, or otherwise produces such a stamped, branded, or marked container, knowing it to be empty or to contain such other contents;
